ai_disclosure: Items in this edition were drafted from hand-written editorial templates. AI rewriting was not applied.

items.headline: Roma Norte rents soften 4 % year-on-year.
items.body: Quarterly figures from one of the larger CDMX coliving operators show a 4 % drop in average monthly rent in Roma Norte and Condesa, attributed to a wave of post-2024 building completions.

items.headline: Temporary Resident visa hits 180-day clearance time.
items.lede: INM workload in DF (CDMX) spiked in March.
items.body: Applicants for the residente temporal visa via the CDMX INM office report processing times of 90–180 days, up from 30–60 in 2024. Applicants outside CDMX (Querétaro, Mérida) report normal turnaround.

items.headline: Canggu council caps new villa permits.
items.lede: No new tourism-zoned licences through Q4.
items.body: The Canggu sub-district council passed a temporary cap on new tourism-zoned villa permits, citing road-traffic and water-supply pressure. Existing rentals continue.

items.headline: Indonesia E33G remote-worker visa fee unchanged.
items.lede: IDR 8.5M for the year. No bump in 2026.
items.body: The Directorate of Immigration confirmed in writing that the E33G visa fee will remain at IDR 8.5M for 2026 fiscal year. The visa is single-entry; multi-entry costs IDR 10.5M.

items.headline: Vake apartment rents tilt 6 % higher year-on-year.
items.lede: Demand from Russian and Iranian arrivals.
items.body: Rental data from a major Tbilisi listings site shows Vake one-bedroom averages up 6 % year-on-year, attributed to a continued inflow of long-stay residents from Russia and Iran. Old town rents are flat.

items.headline: Visa-free 365 days unchanged for most passports.
items.lede: Reciprocity with KR, JP, EU steady.
items.body: The Georgian government re-confirmed its 365-day visa-free policy for citizens of 95 countries through 2027, including South Korea, Japan, and the Schengen 27. Continuous 365 days are not interruptable by short exits.
